$comment: c106 pe: stdin/stdout/stderr = MACROS -> (&__iob_func()[N]) (the msvcrt FILE-array convention; a FUNCTION import that sidesteps the D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT pe wall; elf DATA symbols stay [elf]) + fgetws/fputws + _wfopen/_wpopen + SEEK_* (0/1/2). | Model 3 (2026-06-09): PLATFORM-NEUTRAL descriptor. `library` is a per-object-format map; the active target's format selects its runtime image at compile_pipeline resolution. The `fseek` offset (param[1]) and `ftell` result are the C `long` type, encoded here in the LP64 (i64) form correct for the runnable elf/macho targets. FC3 c1: the Windows LLP64 (i32) form for these 2 is now declared per-symbol via signatureByDataModel (the active format dataModel selects it; D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH). ★ c82 (D-FFI-SHELL-STDIO-SEMANTIC-SURFACE): (1) `typedefs` ships FILE as an opaque named struct `struct "FILE" { arr<u64, 6> }` — the EXACT TypeId every signature below already spells (★ c109: SIZED to 48B = the x64 msvcrt legacy `_iobuf` {_ptr,_cnt,_base,_flag,_file,_charbuf,_bufsiz,_tmpfname}, verified against MinGW-w64 stdio.h; was the zero-size `{}` — pe `__iob_func()[N]` [shell.c:160 `#define stdout (&__iob_func()[1])`] needs the STRIDE. The single opaque `arr<u64,6>` field keeps `.member` fail-loud; FILE is only ever ptr<FILE> [never by-value] so the 48B is INERT on elf/macho where it is never materialized) (interner-deduped), the dirent.json DIR precedent: `FILE *f` resolves in type position (+ the parse oracle steers block-scope `FILE *in;` declarations), member access on it fails loud. size_t rides too — C89 7.19.1 REQUIRES stdio.h to provide size_t + NULL without stddef.h (sqlite's shell.c never includes stddef.h); u64 = the stddef.json width (LP64+LLP64 agree), first-wins dedup makes double-provision with stddef/string/stdlib/time moot. (2) the printf family ships as VARIADIC externs (`fn(..., ...)`) — the c14c fcntl/ioctl mechanism; the README's variadic-exclusion note predates c14c and is updated. vfprintf's third param is spelled `va_list` — resolved by the reader's named-type bindings (threaded from the semantic tier's per-CC va_list mint, D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VA-LIST-TYPE) so the descriptor stays arch-NEUTRAL while the param lands the ABI-exact type (SysV: __va_list_tag[1]; AAPCS64: the 32B __va_list struct; Win64: char*) — byte-identical to a user-written `extern int vfprintf(FILE*, const char*, va_list);`. (3) popen/pclose are NOT shipped: shell.c self-declares popen/pclose at file scope (goal-2: the user decl wins + FF5 binds the default libc); shipping them un-consumed would be over-ship — additive when a real consumer lands. ★ TF-C27 (D-SHIPPED-SYMBOL-PER-TARGET-AVAILABILITY, the unistd.json fdatasync/fallocate precedent): fileno IS now shipped — `int fileno(FILE*)` (glibc /usr/include/stdio.h:883) — because a real consumer landed: sqlite src/test_quota.c calls it on the POSIX path (fsync(fileno(p->f)) :15792, ftruncate(fileno(...)) :15853, fstat(fileno(...)) :15877/:15894). Gated per-symbol availableObjectFormats:[elf] — need-driven (the sole consumer is the elf testfixture) AND eager-import-safe: glibc exports it WEAK (`W fileno@@GLIBC_2.2.5` x86_64 + `W fileno@@GLIBC_2.17` arm64, nm -D libc.so.6 — present=resolvable at load, the W-is-safe rule), so both elf run legs bind it. pe + macho are DEFERRED (fail loud S0001 until a consumer lands there): on pe the C name `fileno` is `#define fileno _fileno` over msvcrt's `_fileno` export, so a bare `fileno` import would break every pe stdio binary's LOAD (D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT) — the pe form needs a `_fileno` symbol + a `fileno`→`_fileno` macro (the __iob_func precedent); macho HAS fileno but has no consumer/run-witness — the stdout elf(c84)→macho(c117) staging precedent. RUN witness: examples/c-subset/shipped_fileno (fileno(stdin/stdout/stderr) == 0/1/2 → exit 42, elf x86_64 + arm64). (4) ★ c84 (D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT, the §B-locked option A): stdout/stderr/stdin ship as extern DATA OBJECTS (`kind: object`, type FILE* — the layout engine derives the 8-byte pointer size/alignment from the DataModel, never hardcoded), gated `availableObjectFormats: ["elf"]` — the ELF exec formats declare `dataImportBinding: copy-relocation` (exec-local .bss slot + DEFINED OBJECT dynsym + R_*_COPY; ld-linux copies libc's object in at startup). NOT pe/macho at c84: their data-import binding models had not landed. ★ c117 (D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT, the got-indirect half): MACHO NOW LANDS — Darwin `#define stdout __stdoutp` (a true extern DATA symbol, no accessor), so macho ships __stdinp/__stdoutp/__stderrp as `kind:object` [macho] (C-name __stdoutp → Mach-O export ___stdoutp) + a macho `macros` variant rewriting stdin/stdout/stderr → __stdinp/__stdoutp/__stderrp (mirrors the pe __iob_func macro). They bind via Mach-O __got non-lazy pointers (macho.format.json dataImportBinding:got-indirect; macho.cpp binds symbolVa[dataExtern] to the __got slot; MIR→LIR lea-of-slot + deref). PE still routes stdout through the __iob_func accessor (its __imp_ data thunk model is unbuilt); a pe reference to stdout stays the accessor macro, not a data object. ★ c85 (shell.c `setvbuf(stderr, 0, _IONBF, 0)`): the setvbuf MODE DOMAIN ships complete (_IOFBF/_IOLBF/_IONBF — the SEEK_*/F_OK closed-domain precedent; setvbuf itself shipped at c82). VALUES DIVERGE per-format: glibc stdio.h 0/1/2 (verified /usr/include/stdio.h:94-96) = Darwin (BSD lineage 0/1/2) but msvcrt 0x0000/0x0040/0x0004 → _IOFBF stays FLAT (0 everywhere, the errno low-block discipline); _IOLBF/_IONBF carry per-format `variants` (the FILENAME_MAX mechanism).
